title: Rocket Lab announces five-launch Neutron deal as it continues aiming for late 2026 debut
text: Rocket Lab announced a five-launch Neutron deal as the company continues aiming for late 2026 debut of its medium-lift rocket. Customers signed multi-launch contracts for Neutron despite the vehicle not yet flying, demonstrating confidence in medium-lift demand. The first Neutron launch is targeted for mid-2025, with multi-launch agreements starting in mid-2026. Q4 2026 remains the first-flight target. Stage 1 tank components using automated fiber placement are progressing through testing. The company signed 31 new Electron and HASTE contracts plus five dedicated Neutron launches during Q1 2026, bringing total contracted missions above 70. The company completed the Mynaric acquisition and entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Motiv Space Systems, strengthening Rocket Lab's vertical integration strategy.
